                       For coverage of series, see footnote 1, table B-2.

                       Spendable earnings are calculated by taking the average weekly pay for all production
                or nonsupervisory jobs, both full-time and part-time, and then deducting social security and
                Federal income taxes applicable to a single worker or to a married worker with three depen-
                dents who earned this amount (see Explanatory Notes for the establishment data in the back o*
                this publication). A technical note on the calculation and uses of the spendable earnings series
                is available on request.

                RELATION BETWEEN THE HOUSEHOLD AND ESTABLISH-
                MENT SERIES                                                                   The household survey measures hours actually worked whereas the
                                                                                           payroll survey measures hours paid for by employers. In the
                  The household and establishment data supplement one another,             household survey data, all persons with a job but not at work are ex-
               each providing significant types of information that the other cannot       cluded from the hours distributions and the computations of average
               suitably supply. Population characteristics, for example, are readily       hours. In the payroll survey, production or nonsupervisory employees
               obtained only from the household survey whereas detailed industrial         on paid vacation, paid holiday, or paid sick leave are included and
               classifications can be reliably derived only from establishment reports.    assigned the number of hours for which they were paid during the
                  Data from these two sources differ from each other because of dif-       reporting period.
               ferences in definition and coverage, sources of information, methods
               of collection, and estimating procedures. Sampling variability and          Earnings

               factors which have a differential effect on levels and trends of the two       The household survey measures median earnings of wage and salary

                Employment                                                                 worker's sole or primary job. Data from the establishment survey

               sample survey—sampling and nonsampling. The standard errors pro-             sample rather than the entire population is surveyed. The sample

               response and enumeration but do not measure any systematic biases in         samples with a known probability. For example, if all possible

                  Over a course of a year, the size of the Nation's labor force, the          All civilian labor force and unemployment rate statistics, as well as

               labor market activity undergo sharp fluctuations due to such seasonal       by aggregating independently adjusted series. For example, for each
